reviews.review_description: Paid for the pro subscription, but never received it. Discord is dead.
As in title I paid for pro and don't get it
Why would you use/install?

its atrociously bad, its a shame to any software and software fields in such. I have to remember if i have seen in past worse software. Best of it, they ask 20 bucks monthly fee one of the most expensive softwares in spite of it alongside too.

It messes and locks your entire desktop. It behaves /pretends like it can map any window anywhere. Btw which oculus desktop can do itself and flawlessly when I think about it  , its also very unintuitive, it can hardly be centered, and windows can be placed only in single stripe which becomes way too curvy depending on your position. Also menus are cluttered and cumbersome

I gave this 5* because it has such potential. I have tried immersed and workgroups both good but I really like how this separates the running apps and drags them into the work areas. It has bugs though, things that would be obvious after 5 minutes of testing so the opinions that this is not being developed seem likely to me. It’s such a shame it has some unique features like passing through the controllers and the locally connected usb mouse into the pc desktop. Sound and mic passthrough actually worked which is closer than workgroups and immersed have got, but I couldn’t stop my voice feeding back into the headset so unusable. A Bluetooth ear piece and mic solved that so not a big deal. It works well in passthrough but if I go immersed the strobing hands on the controllers are a big distraction therefore unusable, the hand control is no replacement. It’s worth a look though to see what virtual computing might be one day.

Update. You can get by with hands so when immersed I put down the controllers to stop the hand strobing and use a Bluetooth mouse that can grab windows and the wheel can zoom in an out. I didn't realise pointing at the apps then making a fist will grab the screen as well. So not using controllers in immersed is actually not a big deal. Its back to my favourite workspace at the moment.

When I first tried it I immediately didn't like it because the work area was to close. I didn't realise you can go into settings and set the work area distance as well as zoom the areas with the controller. Its set far too close on the first run so its easy to get fed up and abandon it. If you try it give it some time to fiddle with the settings its worth it in my opinion

This was a great app with great potential but for whatver reason they stopped pushing any new developments. I was on the discord channel but left becauae there was no transperancy, people would ask about VR app dates and beyond vague "in the works" type comments, they would just ignore the question. The only work they would report on is the 2D app. Now the discord link 404s.

It would be nice if vSpatial would communicate their intentions but they aren't and now that Immersed has bluetooth keyboard and mouse support, I dont care anymore. I am done with vSpatial and cancelling my subscription.
I love this app. I use it all the time.
Won't work
They used an apple vision pro in the vid I swear 🤣🤣🤣

It has good features but one major issue, that should be easy to fix though:
The hight of the monitors can be adjusted, but only from very low to low, which means you are kind of looking down most of the time. The is a free float mode, but in that one the center point of the screen rotation is also rather around my hips than around my head, which again causes you to look down in a relative position.

developer_response.body:
Hey Stevo776,

Update again: We just pushed a new build that has much higher screen size limits, curious if you can try it out and let me know if this addresses your needs.

Original Response:
Thanks for trying out vSpatial. Feedback like this helps us better prioritize our list, so I've added in your comments and we plan on getting an update out soon that will let you move the screens higher. We'd love to hear additional thoughts you may have on improving the platform.

-Daniel @ vSpatial Dev Team

reviews.review_description:
This app gives you a virtual screen for each program you are running on your PC! Such a great productivity boost! Add to that that you can pull screens from two PC's at the same time. This app is next level!

It even provides a virtual web cam so you can attend Teams and Zoom meetings (with your Ready Player Me avatar) from inside VR.

Edit: Two PC connections are back. Yes! This is one of my favourite features because I normally work with two PCs. Also, the web-based remote is really nice touch, adding greatly to the utility of the system even outside VR.

The one thing I can think of that would make this even better would be the ability to use vSpactial from within Quest Home. That said, I did try the web-based remote. It worked, but the screens are unreadable. Oh well. Still a fantastic piece of software!

Had to install parallels on my Mac to use it with Windows. Not a big deal since I’m a programmer and needed it anyway. Once it was up and running I was writing and debugging code on 3 huge monitors with email on a fourth. Only a few rough edges before this is main stream like needing special keyboard and mouse. I still plug in and work about 2-3 hours a week from it. I think there is more I can do but the usage information is tough to find.

This is a great app for bringing your PC screen and apps into VR. Takes all your apps and converts them to discrete windows. Remember this is from a device with one physical screen and in VR i typically have 8. Helps organize your work efforts and surrounds you with your apps/windows so you can quickly get to what you need. Go into focus mode to concentrate on that one app or document and remove all the other distractions. I work in VR several hours a week using vSpatial and love it.  
To have meetings with those on PC, they will need the free workstation app from www.vspatial.com to join you. Once they have it they can join you from the desktop while you are in VR.
The negative reviews i am seeing seem really petty. It doesn't support Linux or Mac so 2 stars? I have latency so 1 star? Look do some research. You need Windows, you need a good connection, you need a decent PC or laptop. I've worked several hours a week for months in vSpatial (as have others, check the Discord) and can testify IT WORKS! Its a different way of working and you have to dedicate yourself to adapting your workflow but once you do, you WILL be more efficient. 
Check out my overview at VR 4 Work on YouTube https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOPivLudusITkFvxUMzu3SA

reviews.review_title: Could get it to work on my Mac Studio M1
reviews.review_description: The concept seems great. From what I could see, it’s visually more appealing to me than immersed, however, I can’t get it to work with my Mac Studio. The pkg downloads and installs just fine, but that’s as far as it goes. I couldn’t launch the remote app. I also upgrade before I started using it, so I kind of regret that, but whatever, I’m sure it’ll work better at some point.
Doesn't work for me.  Setup went ok, when I try to connect to me PC, I hear my windows ding that something has connected, and another quick ding that it has disconnected.

developer_response.body:
Hey TruFooser,

Are both the headset and your remote on the same network? Is the headset on a 2g or 5g wifi connection? A couple things that MAY help are 1. After installing the vSpatial remote, you may need to restart your PC (it depends on if we needed to update some Windows C++ redistributables or not). 2. Are you behind any firewalls that may be prohibiting the connection?

If these don't work, please right click vSpatial in the bottom right app trap and select "support" and then send a ticket and "attach log files". This will help us diagnose the issue further.

-Daniel from the vSpatial Dev Team

reviews.review_description: How to track my keyboards in the app? I tried several times but cannot do. Is there any special keyboards which could be tracked? Or like any laptop can tracking it? Mouse is working well but keyboards no. Also for the passthrough, how to move to the point where my keyboards is located?

reviews.review_title: Unsure how to download after purchase
reviews.review_description: It says I purchased it and I followed the directions on the oculus but I am unsure how to download it

developer_response.body:
Hey Benitez2,

On the PC you wish to connect to, go to vspatial.com/downloads and install the latest "Remote Desktop Utility". Once you've installed on your PC, use the same email to login that you used to login to our app on the Quest. Once that's done, you should see your PC as an available PC in the headset. Happy to walk you through it if you'd like. Feel free to send me an email daniel@vspatial.com . 

We are working on creating more tutorial videos that'll help but those aren't ready for release yet.

-Daniel @ vSpatial Dev Team

reviews.review_description: Interesting take on productivity in VR. I’ve recently installed the app and still trying some of the features. So far, very happy.

developer_response.body:
Hey erlkay,

Thanks for the kind review. We'd love to hear what feature requests/ enhancements you may have so we can continue to make vSpatial a good experience. I'd love to hear more about your experience. Feel free to reach out to me at anytime daniel@vspatial.com . 

Thanks again for checking vSpatial out!

-Daniel @ vSpatial Dev Team

reviews.review_description: This app is awesome!  I can leave my computer at home now and travel and still have full access to my applications with large monitors.   So impressive!
Well I downloaded the software and all it says is purchased and I cannot find it on my computer...not user friendly at all

developer_response.body:
Hey bishopptv,

Once installed on your remote PC, you should see vSpatial in the bottom right App tray (sometimes it's in the additional app tray section by clicking the ^ next to your clock) on Windows 10 & 11. Make sure you're logged in there and that once logged in, it should say "Available" and have a green icon. We're working on additional tutorial videos that'll be up soon. Feel free to reach out to me if you need additional help getting things setup (daniel@vspatial.com)

-Daniel @ vSpatial Dev Team
